data.content:
Here are **three evaluation-phase performance issues** in this project:

---

### 1. Deep Import Chain (`level1 → level2 → level3`)

Line 6 of `EvalHeavy.csproj` imports `level1.props`, which imports `level2.props`, which imports `level3.props`. Each nested `<Import>` adds an evaluation pass — MSBuild must locate, parse, and evaluate every file in the chain before any target can execute. Deep import trees (especially beyond ~20 levels) measurably slow evaluation. **Flatten these into a single file or reduce nesting.**

### 2. Overly Broad Glob Pattern

```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.*" Exclude="**\bin\**;**\obj\**" />
```

`**\*.*` walks the **entire directory tree** during evaluation — including `.git/`, `node_modules/`, and any other large folders. This glob expansion happens every time the project is evaluated (builds, IDE loads, etc.). **Restrict the glob to specific extensions or directories**, or add costly directories to `$(DefaultItemExcludes)`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <DefaultItemExcludes>$(DefaultItemExcludes);node_modules\**;.git\**</DefaultItemExcludes>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### 3. File I/O Property Function During Evaluation

```xml
<BuildNotes>$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('build-notes.txt'))</BuildNotes>
```

Property functions execute during the **evaluation phase**, not the execution phase. `File::ReadAllText` performs disk I/O every time the project is evaluated. This blocks the MSBuild evaluation pipeline. **Move file reads into a custom target** with `Inputs/Outputs` so the read only happens during execution and only when the file changes:

```xml
<Target Name="ReadBuildNotes" BeforeTargets="Build"
        Inputs="build-notes.txt" Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-notes.cache">
  <ReadLinesFromFile File="build-notes.txt">
    <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildNotesLines"/>
  </ReadLinesFromFile>
</Target>
```

---

**Key concept:** MSBuild evaluation (property/item/import expansion) runs before any targets execute. All three issues above add work to evaluation, which runs on every build, IDE open, and design-time build — making them especially costly.
